1: But let thine heart keep my commandments:
2: And peace, shall they add to thee.
3: Write them upon the table of thine heart:
4: In the sight of God and man.
5: And lean not unto thine own understanding.
6: And he shall direct thy paths.
7: Fear the LORD LORD , and depart from evil.
9: And with the firstfruits of all thine increase:
10: And thy presses shall burst out with new wine.
11: Neither be weary of his correction:
12: Even as a father the son in whom in whom he delighteth.
13: And the man that that getteth understanding.
14: And the gain thereof than fine gold.
15: And all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared unto her.
16: And And in her left hand riches and honour.
17: And all her paths are are peace.
18: And happy is every one is every one that retaineth her.
19: By understanding hath he established the heavens.
20: And the clouds drop down the dew.
23: And thy foot shall not stumble.
24: Yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet.
25: Neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it cometh.
26: And shall keep thy foot from being taken.
27: When it is in the power of thine hand to do it it .
28: And to morrow I will give; when thou hast it by thee.
29: Seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.
30: If he have done thee no harm.
31: And choose none of his ways.
32: But his secret is is with the righteous.
33: But he blesseth the habitation of the just.
34: But he giveth grace unto the lowly.
35: But shame shall be the promotion of fools.
